commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Policy plugin secret provider/SecretRef and auth-profile conformance evidence, doctor/lint findings, docs, focused tests, and a test-runtime health-registry reset export.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this PR adds a new Policy conformance category. Source inspection shows current main lacks the secret/auth check ids, while the PR head adds them and focused regression coverage.
commands.review_followup: No narrow automated repair is indicated; the blocker is contributor final-head proof plus maintainer/security owner acceptance.

target.title: Policy: add secret and auth conformance checks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds Policy 1.0 config conformance checks for OpenClaw config secret provider posture, config SecretRef provenance, and config auth-profile metadata/mode posture.

Logical base: #80783 after the signed oc-path evidence fix. This PR intentionally skips #81104 and does not include runtime audit, gateway protocol, approval metadata, Swift protocol, or before-tool-call changes.

- Enforces `secrets.requireManagedProviders`, `secrets.denySources`, and `secrets.allowInsecureProviders` against OpenClaw config secret providers and SecretRefs.
- Adds config auth-profile metadata/mode checks with `auth.profiles.requireMetadata` and `auth.profiles.allowModes`.
- Keeps secret/auth evidence out of attestations unless the policy contains secret/auth rules, preserving existing hashes for channel/MCP/model/network/tool-only policies.
- Documents the config-level policy syntax and JSON evidence shape.

## Policy syntax

```jsonc
{
  "secrets": {
    "requireManagedProviders": true,
    "denySources": ["exec"],
    "allowInsecureProviders": false,
  },
  "auth": {
    "profiles": {
      "requireMetadata": ["provider", "mode"],
      "allowModes": ["api_key", "token"],
    },
  },
}
```

## Scope

This is config-level conformance only. `doctor --lint` and `policy check` read existing OpenClaw config and report whether configured settings conform to the authored policy.

This PR does not mutate config, contact secret providers, read secret values, scan arbitrary config values for secrets, attest per-agent credential stores such as `auth-profiles.json`, change credential resolution, or add runtime enforcement. More policy work can cover additional secrets-regulation surfaces later, but this PR keeps secrets management owned by the existing auth and credential flows.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: adds policy conformance evidence and findings for configured secret provider/SecretRef posture and config auth-profile metadata/mode posture, without taking on general secrets management or runtime enforcement.

Real environment tested: WSL checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-81974-policy` on Ubuntu-24.04 after rebase onto current `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts --run --reporter=dot`
- `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-policy-81974-no-inline.tsbuildinfo`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md`
- `git diff --check`
- `AUTOREVIEW_OUTPUT=/tmp/autoreview-81974-after-compat1.log /mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch`
- `AUTOREVIEW_OUTPUT=/tmp/autoreview-81974-after-compat2.log /mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch`

Evidence after fix: focused policy doctor/CLI tests passed 2 files and 92 tests. Extension test typecheck, bundled extension lint, docs MDX, diff whitespace, and two consecutive autoreview passes were clean. Head commit `d72baa8115` is signed and locally verified with `git log -1 --show-signature`.

Observed result after fix: policy secret/auth checks now report config conformance for managed providers, denied provider sources, insecure providers, auth-profile metadata, and auth-profile modes. Channel/MCP/model/network/tool-only attestations do not include unrelated secret/auth evidence and do not drift because of unrelated secret/auth config changes.

What was not tested: no live external vault or credential provider was contacted. No runtime enforcement path was exercised because this PR is limited to policy/doctor config conformance.

## Related

- #80783 - Policy: add model, network, and MCP conformance checks
- #81981 - Policy: add gateway exposure checks
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
